questions: What is the hard problem of consciousness?
answers: Artificial consciousness refers to the hypothetical creation of machines that have subjective experiences and self-awareness. While current AI can mimic human responses, there's no evidence any AI system is truly conscious.

The hard problem of consciousness asks why physical processes in the brain create subjective experience. Until we solve this for biological brains, creating artificial consciousness remains a distant and uncertain goal.
Artificial consciousness holo machine e chetona ba self-awareness toiri korar concept. Ekhonkar AI onek kichu korte pare, kintu shotti kore feel kore ba bujhe kina seta ekta boro proshno.

Current AI systems, including large language models, process information and generate responses but don't have subjective experiences or feelings. Whether machines could ever truly be conscious is one of the deepest questions in philosophy and computer science.
